drugs.notes: 150mg twice daily days 1-28. Dose reductions: first reduction to 100mg twice daily, second reduction to 50mg twice daily. Interacts with CYP3A4 agents - always check interactions. If patient vomits or misses a dose, do not take extra dose that day.
1mg once daily days 1-28. No dose reductions required for anastrozole due to myelosuppression.

dose_modifications.notes: Suspend dose until toxicity resolves to NCI grade 2 or less. Dose reduction is not required.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological toxicity Grade 3 recurrent or Grade 4
dose_modifications.notes: Suspend dose until toxicity resolves to NCI grade 2 or less. Resume at next lower dose level.
Suspend dose for at least 48 hours after last growth factor dose and until toxicity resolves to NCI grade 2 or less. Resume at next lower dose unless dose was already reduced for that toxicity.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic toxicity persistent or recurrent Grade 2 or Grade 3 (ALT/AST)
dose_modifications.notes: Suspend dose until toxicity resolves to baseline or Grade 1. Resume at next lower dose.
Discontinue abemaciclib.
Administer with caution in patients with severe renal impairment.
No data for severe renal impairment. Use with caution and close monitoring for signs of toxicity.

dose_modifications.condition: Diarrhoea Grade 2 not resolving within 24 hours
dose_modifications.notes: Suspend dose until resolution. Dose reduction is not required.

dose_modifications.condition: Diarrhoea Grade 2 persisting or recurring despite maximal supportive measures, or Grade 3/4
dose_modifications.notes: Suspend dose until toxicity resolves to Grade 1 or less. Resume at next lower dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological toxicity (not diarrhoea or liver) persistent or recurrent Grade 2 not resolving within 7 days, or Grade 3/4
dose_modifications.notes: Suspend dose until toxicity resolves to Grade 1 or less. Resume at next lower dose.

pre_medications: Loperamide 4mg after first loose stool then 2mg after each subsequent loose stool up to maximum 16mg in 24 hours (take home)
special_warnings: CDK4/6 inhibitor: Abemaciclib interacts with many agents especially CYP3A4 inhibitors/inducers - always check drug interactions.
Monitor for hepatotoxicity: LFTs at baseline, every 2 weeks for first 2 months, monthly for next 2 months, then as indicated.
FBC, LFTs and U&Es at baseline then every 2 weeks for first 8 weeks, then every 4 weeks for further 8 weeks, then minimum every 12 weeks.
Treatment breaks of up to 6 weeks allowed to allow toxicities to settle.
In pre- or perimenopausal women, endocrine therapy must be combined with LHRH agonist (not included in ARIA regimen).
If third dose reduction necessary, treatment should be stopped.
Follow National Patient Safety Alert on oral chemotherapy (NPSA/2008/RRR001).
Abemaciclib should only be prescribed under supervision of a consultant oncologist.
Maximum of 12 cycles set in ARIA but treatment continues until disease progression or intolerance.

drugs.notes: 150mg twice daily days 1-28. Dose reductions: first reduction to 100mg twice daily, second reduction to 50mg twice daily. If patient vomits or misses a dose, do not take extra dose that day.

Cycle 1: 500mg on days 1 and 15. Cycle 2 onwards: 500mg on day 1 only. Administered as two consecutive 5ml slow IM injections (1-2 min/injection), one in each buttock. Caution at dorsogluteal site due to proximity to sciatic nerve.

dose_modifications.notes: Suspend dose until toxicity resolves to NCI grade 2 or less. Dose reduction is not required.

dose_modifications.notes: Suspend dose until toxicity resolves to NCI grade 2 or less. Resume at next lower dose level.
Suspend dose for at least 48 hours after last growth factor dose and until toxicity resolves to NCI grade 2 or less. Resume at next lower dose unless dose was already reduced.

dose_modifications.notes: Suspend dose until toxicity resolves to baseline or Grade 1. Resume at next lower dose.
Discontinue abemaciclib.
Caution advised in moderate to severe hepatic impairment.
Administer with caution in severe renal impairment.
No data for severe renal impairment. Use with caution and close monitoring.

dose_modifications.notes: Suspend dose until resolution. Dose reduction not required.

dose_modifications.notes: Suspend dose until toxicity resolves to Grade 1 or less. Resume at next lower dose.

special_warnings: CDK4/6 inhibitor: Abemaciclib interacts with many agents especially CYP3A4 - always check drug interactions.
Monitor for hepatotoxicity: LFTs at baseline, every 2 weeks for first 2 months, monthly for next 2 months, then as indicated.

Treatment breaks of up to 6 weeks allowed solely to allow toxicities to settle.
In pre- or perimenopausal women, ovarian ablation or suppression with LHRH agonist is mandatory (not included in ARIA regimen).
Cycle 1 fulvestrant given on days 1 AND 15; from cycle 2 onwards given on day 1 only.
If third dose reduction necessary, treatment should be stopped.
Follow National Patient Safety Alert on oral chemotherapy (NPSA/2008/RRR001).

drugs.notes: 150mg twice daily days 1-28. Dose reductions: first reduction to 100mg twice daily, second reduction to 50mg twice daily. If patient vomits or misses a dose, do not take extra dose that day.
2.5mg once daily days 1-28. No dose reductions required for letrozole due to myelosuppression.

Administer letrozole with caution in severe renal impairment.

dose_modifications.notes: Suspend dose until resolution. Dose reduction not required.

dose_modifications.notes: Suspend dose until toxicity resolves to Grade 1 or less. Resume at next lower dose.

special_warnings: CDK4/6 inhibitor: Abemaciclib interacts with many agents especially CYP3A4 - always check drug interactions.
Monitor for hepatotoxicity: LFTs at baseline, every 2 weeks for first 2 months, monthly for next 2 months, then as indicated.
FBC, LFTs and U&Es at baseline then every 2 weeks for first 8 weeks, then every 4 weeks for further 8 weeks, then minimum every 12 weeks.
Treatment breaks of up to 6 weeks allowed solely to allow toxicities to settle.
In pre- or perimenopausal women, endocrine therapy must be combined with LHRH agonist (not included in ARIA regimen).

drugs.notes: Cycles 1-4 only (Phase 1). Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands (20mg/mL). Reduce to 75% if CrCl 10-20ml/min; 50% if CrCl <10ml/min.

Cycles 1-4 only (Phase 1). Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands (2mg/mL). Maximum cumulative lifetime dose 900mg/m². Vesicant - follow extravasation protocol. Discontinue if cardiac failure develops.

Cycles 5-8 only (Phase 2). IV infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 180 minutes. Must be administered via non-PVC set with in-line 0.22 micron filter. Dose banded (6mg/mL). Vesicant. Blood pressure and pulse monitored half-hourly during infusion. Hepatic dose reductions: bilirubin <26 umol/L = 135mg/m²; 27-51 umol/L = 75mg/m²; >51 umol/L = 50mg/m².

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 80% of original dose after NCI-CTC grade 3+ haematological event. If second episode despite dose reduction, consider changing or stopping therapy.
Reduce to 80% of original dose after NCI-CTC grade 3+ haematological event.
Reduce to 80% of original dose after NCI-CTC grade 3+ haematological event.
Reduce to 75% of original dose.
Reduce to 50% of original dose.
Dose reduce in severe impairment only.
Give 50% of original dose. Also applies if AST 2-4x ULN or bilirubin 21-51 umol/L.
Give 25% of original dose. Also applies if AST >4x ULN or bilirubin >51 umol/L.
Contra-indicated.
Withhold paclitaxel until neuropathy recovers to NCI-CTC grade 1 then reduce to 75% of original dose.
Withhold until resolves to NCI-CTC grade 1 then reduce to 50% of original dose. Discontinue if neuropathy does not resolve to grade 1.

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to cyclophosphamide/epirubicin (cycles 1-4)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to cyclophosphamide/epirubicin (cycles 1-4)
Dexamethasone 4mg twice daily oral for 3 days starting day 2 of cycle (take home, cycles 1-4)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral (take home, cycles 1-4)
Ondansetron 8mg twice daily oral for 3 days starting evening of day 1 (take home, cycles 1-4)
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV 30 minutes prior to paclitaxel (cycles 5-8)
Dexamethasone 20mg IV 30 minutes prior to paclitaxel (cycles 5-8)
H2 antagonist per local formulary 30 minutes prior to paclitaxel (cycles 5-8): ranitidine 50mg IV or famotidine 20mg oral or nizatidine 150mg oral or ranitidine 150mg oral
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to paclitaxel (cycles 5-8)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral (take home, cycles 5-8)
G-CSF: filgrastim 30MU SC daily for 5 days from day 3, OR lenograstim 33.6MU SC daily for 5 days from day 3, OR pegfilgrastim/lipegfilgrastim 6mg SC on day 2 (all cycles)
special_warnings: Regimen is two sequential phases: Cyclophosphamide + Epirubicin x4 cycles (14-day), THEN Paclitaxel x4 cycles (14-day). Total 8 cycles.
Epirubicin: maximum cumulative lifetime dose is 900mg/m². Monitor LVEF at baseline especially in patients with cardiac history.
Ensure adequate cardiac function before starting epirubicin. Discontinue if cardiac failure develops.
Epirubicin is a vesicant - follow extravasation guidelines.
Paclitaxel is a vesicant - follow extravasation guidelines.
Paclitaxel must be administered via non-PVC administration set with in-line 0.22 micron filter.

Hypersensitivity reactions to paclitaxel tend to occur with first or second infusion. Interrupt for minor symptoms; immediately discontinue for severe reactions (profound hypotension, bronchospasm, generalised erythema).
Blood pressure and pulse monitored half-hourly during paclitaxel infusion.
Growth factors (G-CSF) required for all cycles due to accelerated schedule.
Baseline LVEF measurement required.

drugs.notes: 1mg once daily days 1-28. No dose reductions required for anastrozole due to myelosuppression.

125mg once daily days 1-21, then 7 days off. Dose reductions: level -1 = 100mg/day, level -2 = 75mg/day. Must be taken with food. Swallow capsules whole, do not chew. If vomit or miss dose, do not take extra dose.

dose_modifications.notes: No change.
Delay one week. When recovered to NCI-CTC grade 2 or below restart at same dose. Consider dose reduction if recovery takes 7 days or longer or recurrent grade 3 neutropenia.
Delay until recovery to NCI-CTC grade 2 or below. Restart at next lower dose level.
Delay until recovery to NCI-CTC grade 2 or below. Restart at next lower dose level.
Delay until recovery to NCI-CTC grade 2 or below. Restart at next lower dose level.
Insufficient data. Administer only after careful consideration of benefits/risks with close monitoring.

dose_modifications.condition: Moderate or severe hepatic impairment (bilirubin >1.5x ULN and any AST)
dose_modifications.notes: Insufficient data. Administer only after careful consideration of benefits/risks with close monitoring.

special_warnings: CDK4/6 inhibitor: Palbociclib interacts with many other agents - always check drug interactions.
FBC, LFTs and U&Es at baseline then day 1 of each cycle. For cycles 1 and 2, FBC also on day 14.
Neutropenia most frequent adverse effect: median onset 15 days (any grade), 28 days (grade 3/4). Median duration of severe neutropenia 7 days.
Increased risk of infection vs aromatase inhibitor alone - warn patients to report fever promptly.
Treatment breaks up to 6 weeks allowed solely to allow toxicities to settle.
In pre- or perimenopausal women, ovarian ablation or suppression with LHRH agonist is mandatory (not included in ARIA regimen).
If third dose reduction necessary, treatment should be stopped.
Follow National Patient Safety Alert on oral chemotherapy (NPSA/2008/RRR001).
Palbociclib should only be prescribed under supervision of a consultant oncologist.
Maximum of 12 cycles set in ARIA but treatment continues until disease progression or intolerance.

drugs.notes: 1mg once daily days 1-28. No dose reductions required for anastrozole due to myelosuppression.

600mg once daily days 1-21, then 7 days off. Dose reductions: level -1 = 400mg/day, level -2 = 200mg/day. Discontinue if 200mg dose not tolerated. Can be taken with or without food. If vomit or miss dose, do not take extra.

dose_modifications.notes: No change.
Delay one week. When recovered to NCI-CTC grade 2 or below restart at same dose. Consider dose reduction if recovery takes 7 days or longer or recurrent grade 3 neutropenia in subsequent cycles.
Delay until recovery to NCI-CTC grade 2 or below. Restart at next lower dose level.
Delay until recovery to NCI-CTC grade 2 or below. Restart at next lower dose level.
Delay treatment until recovery to NCI-CTC grade 2 or below, then resume at same dose level. If grade 2 recurs, resume at lower dose level.
Interrupt dose until recovery to NCI-CTC grade 2 or below then resume at next lowest dose level. Discontinue if NCI-CTC toxicity recurs.

dose_modifications.condition: ALT/AST >3x ULN combined with total bilirubin >2x ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue ribociclib irrespective of baseline grade.

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological or cardiac toxicity NCI-CTC Grade 3
dose_modifications.notes: Dose interruption until recovery to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below, then resume at same dose level. If grade 3 recurs, resume at next lower dose level.

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological or cardiac toxicity NCI-CTC Grade 4
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue ribociclib.
Interrupt dose. If resolves to <481msec, resume at same dose. If QTcF >=481msec recurs, interrupt until <481msec then resume at next lower dose level.

dose_modifications.condition: QTcF >500msec on at least 2 separate ECGs
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt ribociclib until QTcF <481msec then resume at next lower dose. If QTcF >500msec or >60msec change from baseline with torsade de pointes or polymorphic VT or serious arrhythmia, permanently discontinue.
Insufficient data. Administer only after careful consideration of benefits/risks with close monitoring.

special_warnings: CDK4/6 inhibitor: Ribociclib requires QTc monitoring - ECG before initiating treatment, repeated at day 14 of cycle 1, at beginning of cycle 2, then as clinically indicated.
QTcF prolongation is a known risk with ribociclib - more frequent ECG monitoring required if QTcF prolongation occurs during treatment.
Monitor for hepatotoxicity: FBC and LFTs at baseline and day 1/14 for cycles 1-2, then day 1 of each cycle.
Ribociclib interacts with many other agents - always check drug interactions.
Increased risk of infection vs aromatase inhibitor alone - warn patients to report fever promptly.
Treatment breaks up to 6 weeks allowed solely to allow toxicities to settle.
In pre- or perimenopausal women, ovarian ablation or suppression with LHRH agonist is mandatory (not included in ARIA regimen).
Discontinue ribociclib if the 200mg dose level is not tolerated.
If third dose reduction necessary, treatment should be stopped.
Follow National Patient Safety Alert on oral chemotherapy (NPSA/2008/RRR001).
Ribociclib should only be prescribed under supervision of a consultant oncologist.
Maximum of 12 cycles set in ARIA but treatment continues until disease progression or intolerance.

drugs.notes: 840mg IV in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes on days 1 and 15. First infusion over 60 minutes; if well tolerated subsequent infusions can be over 30 minutes. No dose reductions permitted - delay or discontinue only. Ensure patient receives atezolizumab Patient Alert Card.

100mg/m² IV infusion over 30 minutes on days 1, 8, 15. Dose banded per national dose bands (paclitaxel albumin). Administer using infusion set with 15 micron filter (NOT <15 micron as this may block). NOT interchangeable with standard paclitaxel. Dose level reductions: full=100mg/m², 1st reduction=75mg/m², 2nd reduction=50mg/m², if further reduction needed=discontinue.

dose_modifications.notes: In principle no dose reductions recommended for atezolizumab. Preference is to delay the dose or discontinue treatment.
Withhold until symptoms resolve and radiographic abnormalities improve. May resume if improves to grade 0-1 within 12 weeks and corticosteroids reduced to <=10mg oral prednisone equivalent/day.
Permanently discontinue atezolizumab. Consider corticosteroids.
Withhold atezolizumab. May resume if improves to grade 0-1 within 12 weeks and corticosteroids reduced to <=10mg/day.
Permanently discontinue atezolizumab.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3-4 serum amylase/lipase or Grade 2-3 pancreatitis (immune-related)
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold atezolizumab. May resume if improves to grade 0-1 within 12 weeks and corticosteroids tapered.
Permanently discontinue atezolizumab.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 2 hepatitis (ALT/AST 3-5x ULN or bilirubin 1.5-3x ULN) persisting 5-7 days
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold and consider corticosteroid. May resume when improves to grade 1 or below within 12 weeks.

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 or above hepatitis (ALT/AST >5x ULN or bilirubin >3x ULN)
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue atezolizumab.
Withhold atezolizumab. Resume when symptoms controlled with thyroid replacement/cabimazole.
Permanently discontinue atezolizumab.
Withhold atezolizumab. Grade 3: may resume if resolved and corticosteroids reduced. Grade 4: permanently discontinue.
Permanently discontinue atezolizumab.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment moderate to severe (bilirubin 1.5-5x ULN, AST <=10x ULN) - metastatic BC
dose_modifications.notes: 20% dose reduction recommended for moderate to severe hepatic impairment in metastatic breast cancer.
Withhold until fever resolves and neutrophil count >=1.5x10^9/L, then resume at next lower dose level.
Withhold until improves to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below, then resume at next lower dose level.
Reduce to next lower dose level. Discontinue if adverse drug reaction persists.

dose_modifications.condition: Gastrointestinal toxicity grade 3 mucositis or diarrhoea
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until improves to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below, then resume at next lower dose level.

pre_medications: Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on days 1, 8, 15
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral (take home, day 1 only)
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV when required for infusion related reactions on days 1 and 15
Hydrocortisone sodium succinate 100mg IV when required for infusion related reactions on days 1 and 15
Paracetamol 1000mg oral when required for infusion related reactions on days 1 and 15
Loperamide 4mg oral initially then 2mg after each loose stool up to maximum 16mg/24 hours when required
special_warnings: Paclitaxel albumin bound (Abraxane/nab-paclitaxel) is NOT interchangeable with standard (solvent-based) paclitaxel - different doses, schedules, and toxicity profiles.
IMMUNOTHERAPY: Atezolizumab dose reductions are NOT permitted. Preference is to delay or discontinue only.
Immune-related adverse reactions can be severe or life-threatening and may involve GI, liver, skin, nervous, endocrine, or other organ systems.

Atezolizumab should be permanently discontinued for: NCI-CTC grade 3-4 pneumonitis or hepatitis; any other life-threatening NCI-CTC grade 4 reaction including colitis and renal impairment; any recurrence of severe or NCI-CTC grade 3 reaction; any persistent NCI-CTC grade 2-3 treatment-related adverse reaction that does not recover to grade 1 or resolve within 12 weeks.
Systemic corticosteroids should be avoided before starting atezolizumab (may interfere with efficacy), but can be used after starting to treat immune-related reactions.
Thyroid function tests required prior to starting treatment and every 6 weeks or if clinically indicated.
Treatment breaks of up to 12 weeks beyond expected 3-weekly cycle length allowed to settle immune toxicities.
Formal medical review scheduled at least by end of first 8 weeks of treatment.
Patients must be given an atezolizumab Patient Alert Card.
FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to days 1, 8, 15 of each cycle.
Administer paclitaxel albumin bound using infusion set incorporating a 15 micron filter (filters <15 micron may block).
Do not use medical devices containing silicone oil (syringes and IV bags) to administer paclitaxel albumin bound.

drugs.notes: 50mg/m² IV bolus over 10 minutes on day 1. Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands. Maximum cumulative lifetime dose 450mg/m². Vesicant - follow extravasation protocol. Discontinue if cardiac failure develops.

75mg/m² IV infusion in 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes on day 1. Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands. In patients with poor bone marrow reserves consider starting dose 55mg/m² with view to increase to 75mg/m² if well tolerated. Doses >190mg must be diluted in 500ml (max concentration 0.74mg/mL). Exfoliant - follow extravasation protocol.
500mg/m² IV bolus over 10 minutes on day 1. Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands. Consider mesna for renal impairment. Reduce to 75% if CrCl 10-20ml/min; 50% if CrCl <10ml/min.

dose_modifications.condition: Febrile neutropenia or neutrophil <0.5x10^9/L or platelets <50x10^9/L for >7 days
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce all agents to 80% of original dose. If neutropenia/thrombocytopenia recurs despite dose reduction, further reduce to 50% or stop treatment.

dose_modifications.condition: Febrile neutropenia or neutrophil <0.5x10^9/L or platelets <50x10^9/L for >7 days
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce all agents to 80% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Febrile neutropenia or neutrophil <0.5x10^9/L or platelets <50x10^9/L for >7 days
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce all agents to 80% of original dose.
Give 50% of dose.
Give 25% of dose.
Omit doxorubicin.
Give 75% of dose.
Give 50% of dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: AST/ALT >=1.5x ULN and Alk Phos >=2.5x ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Give 75% of dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment: bilirubin >ULN and/or AST/ALT >=3.5x ULN and Alk Phos >=6x ULN
dose_modifications.notes: Not recommended.
Reduce to 75%. Consider mesna.
Reduce to 50%. Consider mesna.

dose_modifications.condition: Peripheral neuropathy NCI-CTC Grade 3 or above
dose_modifications.notes: Dose reduction to 80% of original dose in the first instance.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC Grade 3 cutaneous toxicity on day 1 of cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolves to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below. Subsequent doses reduced to 80% of original. Discontinue if no recovery after 2 weeks or NCI-CTC grade 4 cutaneous toxicity.
Discontinue doxorubicin if cardiac failure develops.

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg twice daily oral starting day before docetaxel (day -1) for 3 days total (to prevent fluid retention and hypersensitivity). On occasions where pre-medication forgotten, give dexamethasone 20mg IV once only bolus.
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes before chemotherapy
Ondansetron 8mg twice daily oral for 3 days starting evening of day 1 (take home)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral (take home)
G-CSF: filgrastim or bioequivalent 300mcg SC daily for 7 days from day 3, OR lenograstim or bioequivalent 263mcg SC daily for 7 days from day 3, OR pegfilgrastim or bioequivalent 6mg SC on day 2
special_warnings: Doxorubicin: maximum cumulative lifetime dose is 450mg/m². Baseline LVEF measurement required especially in patients with cardiac history.
Doxorubicin is a vesicant - follow extravasation guidelines. Discontinue if cardiac failure develops.

Docetaxel: hypersensitivity reactions tend to occur with first or second infusion. Do not interrupt for minor symptoms (flushing, localised rash). Immediately discontinue for severe reactions (profound hypotension, bronchospasm, generalised erythema).
Docetaxel-induced fluid retention can lead to weight gain - this is NOT a reason to alter doses.
Excessive tearing/lacrimation related to cumulative docetaxel doses (median 400mg/m²) - treat with hypromellose 0.3% eye drops. If ocular irritation continues, reduce docetaxel to 80%.

In patients with poor bone marrow reserves (extensive prior treatment, bone metastases, extensive skeletal radiation) consider starting docetaxel at 55mg/m² with view to increase to 75mg/m² if well tolerated.
Growth factor (G-CSF) support mandatory.
Mouthwashes per local/national mucositis policy.
FBC, U&Es and LFTs prior to each cycle.
Ensure adequate cardiac function before starting treatment. Baseline LVEF should be measured.
